summary: Matched the common fighter ShieldBreakStand action file by replacing MWERKS_GEKKO inline assembly with C for ftCo_80098F3C, ftCo_ShieldBreakStand_Anim, ftCo_ShieldBreakStand_IASA, ftCo_ShieldBreakStand_Phys, and ftCo_ShieldBreakStand_Coll. The PR also tightened the ftCo_80099010 declaration in ft_08A4.h from an unknown placeholder signature to void ftCo_80099010(ftCo_GObj* gobj). The included report shows the file and all listed functions at 100.00%.

key_files.reason: Main matched source file; removed inline assembly and implemented ShieldBreakStand state callbacks in C.
Updated ftCo_80099010 prototype from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS placeholder to a typed ftCo_GObj* function declaration.

what_changed: Removed local float constants ftCo_804D8668 and ftCo_804D866C that represented 0 and 1 in the old assembly path, replacing their uses with direct C arguments NULL, 0, 1, and 0 to Fighter_ChangeMotionState.

Reimplemented ftCo_80098F3C in C: reads ftCo_Fighter from gobj->user_data, selects ftCo_MS_ShieldBreakStandU when the current motion is ftCo_MS_ShieldBreakDownU, otherwise selects ftCo_MS_ShieldBreakStandD, then calls Fighter_ChangeMotionState.
Encoded the motion flags as named bitwise OR values: Ft_MF_KeepColAnimHitStatus | Ft_MF_SkipModel | Ft_MF_SkipMatAnim | Ft_MF_SkipColAnim, corresponding to the immediate 0x1094 used in assembly.
Reimplemented ftCo_ShieldBreakStand_Anim in C as a simple end-of-animation transition: if ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining(gobj) is false, call ftCo_80099010(gobj).
Kept ftCo_ShieldBreakStand_IASA as an empty callback, but changed its parameter type to ftCo_GObj* for consistency with the file's other callbacks.
Reimplemented ftCo_ShieldBreakStand_Phys as a direct wrapper around ft_80084F3C(gobj).
Reimplemented ftCo_ShieldBreakStand_Coll as a direct wrapper around ft_80083F88(gobj).
Added include of ft/forward.h, likely to supply forward declarations such as ftCo_GObj or related fighter types needed by the new C signatures.
Changed ftCo_80099010 declaration in ft_08A4.h from /* static */ UNK_RET ftCo_80099010(UNK_PARAMS) to void ftCo_80099010(ftCo_GObj* gobj).
smart_moves: Used named motion IDs instead of raw constants 0xCF, 0xD1, and 0xD2, making the state transition readable while preserving the branch behavior.
Replaced the raw 0x1094 motion flag immediate with documented Ft_MF_* flag names, which is reusable for future Fighter_ChangeMotionState matches.
Recognized that the physics and collision callbacks were pure tail-style wrappers around existing helper functions, allowing very small C implementations to match.
Typed ftCo_80099010 in the shared header so ftCo_ShieldBreakStand_Anim could call it with a proper ftCo_GObj* argument instead of relying on UNK placeholders.
Kept the no-op IASA callback explicit rather than deleting it, preserving the expected action callback table behavior.
decomp_lessons: For common fighter action-state files, many animation callbacks follow the pattern: call ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining(gobj), and when it returns false, transition via a nearby ftCo_* helper.
Raw motion-state IDs in assembly can often be recovered by comparing against nearby ftCo_MS_* enum names; here ShieldBreakDownU maps to ShieldBreakStandU, otherwise ShieldBreakStandD.
Fighter_ChangeMotionState calls should be expressed with named Ft_MF_* flags once the immediate value is understood, because this both documents intent and helps future matching.
Simple phys/coll callbacks may compile cleanly as one-call wrappers to existing fighter helpers such as ft_80084F3C and ft_80083F88.
When replacing inline asm with C, update shared headers from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to the inferred signature at the same time to avoid preserving unnecessary unknowns.
This PR demonstrates that tiny action-state callback files can be fully matched by identifying state selection, end-of-animation behavior, and one-call physics/collision delegates.
naming_conventions: Common fighter action callbacks use the ftCo_ShieldBreakStand_* naming pattern for Anim, IASA, Phys, and Coll.
The local transition helper kept its address-derived name ftCo_80098F3C because no semantic name was introduced in the PR.
Motion enum names follow ftCo_MS_ShieldBreakDownU, ftCo_MS_ShieldBreakStandU, and ftCo_MS_ShieldBreakStandD.
Fighter motion flag constants use the Ft_MF_* prefix, such as Ft_MF_KeepColAnimHitStatus, Ft_MF_SkipModel, Ft_MF_SkipMatAnim, and Ft_MF_SkipColAnim.
The typed fighter object aliases in this area use ftCo_GObj* for game object callbacks and ftCo_Fighter* for fighter user_data.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted the assembly compare against motion_id 0xCF into a C ternary selecting between the up and down ShieldBreakStand motion states.
Matched the assembly lfs constants 0.0 and 1.0 by passing equivalent literal arguments to Fighter_ChangeMotionState rather than retaining file-scope float constants.
Matched branch-on-animation-remaining assembly with the idiomatic C condition if (!ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining(gobj)) { ftCo_80099010(gobj); }.
Removed MWERKS_GEKKO asm blocks after confirming equivalent C matched all listed functions at 100.00%.
Used direct wrapper functions for callbacks whose assembly only called a single helper, avoiding overcomplication.
follow_up_queries: Look for other ftCommon ShieldBreak action files with callbacks that still use MWERKS_GEKKO asm and may share the same ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining transition pattern.
Search for remaining UNK_RET or UNK_PARAMS declarations around ftCo action helpers in ft_08A4.h that can be typed from matched call sites.
Search for other Fighter_ChangeMotionState calls using raw 0x1094 or equivalent flag combinations that should be expressed as Ft_MF_* names.
